title: API versioning strategy
tagline: Introduce /v1/ URL prefixes and a deprecation header so consumers can migrate safely.
effort: M — router changes + middleware + docs update
risk: Medium (affects all API consumers; phased rollout recommended)
headline: All 47 existing routes currently live at /api/* with no version segment. Adding /v1/ prefix behind a compatibility layer lets old clients keep working while new clients opt in — no big-bang migration needed.

decisions.help: Unversioned routes (/api/foo) still serve ~3 000 req/day from legacy clients. A redirect keeps them alive with zero consumer changes; a 410 forces them to update immediately.

options.label: Keep /api/* and /v1/* running in parallel indefinitely
Return 410 Gone immediately on old paths (breaking)

decisions.help: The Deprecation header (RFC 8594) lets clients log warnings automatically.

options.label: Add Deprecation + Sunset headers on all /api/* responses (recommended)
Include a _deprecated flag in every JSON response body
No active signalling — just update the docs
